components.title: compliant-s3
components.description: Reusable Terraform pattern for an AWS S3 primary bucket plus a dedicated access-log bucket. Hardcodes server-side encryption, versioning, public access block, access logging, and required compliance tags.
components.purpose: Provide a compliant-by-default S3 primitive that any team can adopt with three lines of consumer Terraform.

control-implementations.description: Implementation of NIST 800-53 Rev 5 controls satisfied by this module.

implemented-requirements.description: AES-256 server-side encryption is enforced via aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration. Hardcoded; consumers cannot override.

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle containing terraform plan.json.

implemented-requirements.description: Public access is blocked on both buckets via aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block. All four flags (block_public_acls, block_public_policy, ignore_public_acls, restrict_public_buckets) are set to true. The module does not expose these flags as variables.

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle. terraform/plan.json contains the four-true public access block.

implemented-requirements.description: Access logs from the primary bucket are routed to a dedicated log bucket via aws_s3_bucket_logging. The log bucket carries its own encryption and public access block.

Four required compliance tags (Project, Environment, ManagedBy, ComplianceScope) are applied via the AWS provider's default_tags block. Variable validation rejects environments outside dev/staging/prod and project names outside the regex.

links.text: Signed pipeline bundle. Conftest output verifies tags_all contains all four required keys.
